In use

q12b.title: Does one of the following scenarios apply to your AI system?
enum: The name or trademark of your organization is put on the AI system.
A substantial modification has been made to the AI system.
The intended purpose of the AI system is modified by your organization.
None of the above.

q12.title: How is the AI system developed or deployed?
enum: We develop and use the AI system ourselves.
We only develop the AI system.
We develop the AI system and others also use it.
We use an externally developed AI system.
We import the AI system from a supplier outside the EU.
We buy the AI system from a supplier within the EU and resell it.
We represent a non-EU person or entity to carry out AI Act obligations.
You are a natural person using an AI system for purely personal non-professional activity.
We develop and use the AI system ourselves.
We only develop the AI system.
We develop the AI system and others also use it.
We use an externally developed AI system.
We import the AI system from a supplier outside the EU.
We buy the AI system from a supplier within the EU and resell it.
We represent a non-EU person or entity to carry out AI Act obligations.
You are a natural person using an AI system for purely personal non-professional activity.

intro.ui:alertDescription:
In this questionnaire:
- A 'Downstream provider' equals a 'Provider'. Only in case an AI model is used, this distinction is relevant (see [Art. 3(68) AI Act](https://ai-act-service-desk.ec.europa.eu/en/ai-act/article-3)). Whether an AI model is used, is determined in questionnaire Risk category.
- A 'Product manufacturer' equals a 'Provider'. Being a product manufacturer is only relevant if: (i) the high-risk AI system relates to products for which the laws in Annex I Section A apply; (ii) the system is placed on the market or put into service together with the product; and (iii) under the name or trademark of the product manufacturer. In this case the product manufacturer is considered to be the provider of the high-risk AI system (see [Art. 25(3) AI Act](https://ai-act-service-desk.ec.europa.eu/en/ai-act/article-25)).

ui:badges.label: Art. 25 AI Act - Responsibilities along the AI value chain

q13.ui:description: An AI system is considered 'in use' as soon as it is actually applied, including in a pilot or testing. A system is 'in use' if it has effect on work processes.

ui:badges.label: Art. 113 AI Act - Entry into force and application
